replies.msg:
唔撚該 推比所有人睇 政府已經開始推緊緊急法 只係佢無出聲咋
推出所有人睇 比家人 比外國人睇!

1.深水埗區,黃大仙同沙田入夜已經戒嚴

2.狗已經有任何既權利去做任何違反基本法及法治既事
2.1:無理搜身 甚至係巴士/狗鐵公共交通工具搜乘客既身
2.2:30人圍毆一個市民,對女性市民性侵
2.3:以甲由形容市民,不論男女老幼:小至12歲
2.4:無搜索令情況下進行私人地方

3.開始限制走資

4.開始想封互聯網

5.限制香港市民離境(陳浩天)

6.限制自由市場貨品進出

推
無公開戒嚴之名
卻暗行戒嚴之實

反欺凌
保護警察兒女 你我有責
＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝＝
各位中小學生 為左反欺凌 保護警察兒女
如果有確鑿證據證明邊位同學老豆老母係警察既話記得要
1.搵人放佢大頭相上黎
2.寫埋住邊區
3.寫埋讀咩學校
等市民可以隨時留意住佢地有冇比人欺凌 係有需要既時候可以保護佢地

因為欺凌係唔局限係校園架
而家市民咁仇警 有人可能會作出自然反應係街上做小動作搞警察兒女
例如:
1.唔小心撞佢出馬路
2.警察兒女衝紅燈個陣唔小心擋住佢唔比佢上行人路
3.唔小心踼/撞佢落樓梯
4.係街拍蚊個陣唔小心打中佢
5.係巴士唔小心擋住佢唔比佢落車
6.係巴士/小巴唔小心唔讓開比佢入去靠窗位坐
7.係巴士/小巴唔小心唔讓開比佢離開個靠窗位
8.係行人路/過馬路個陣唔小心"KICK"跌佢
9.唔小心擋住LIP口唔比佢入升降機
10.警察兒女趕返學搭LIP個陣 唔小心撳哂咁多層
11.係佢要上車/追車個陣唔小心扮健身撚當街捉住佢扮SALE野唔比佢上車
12.係街上唔小心撞佢落花槽
13.唔小心撞佢部手機落地
14.唔小心撞佢手上拎住既其他野落地
15.唔小心倒瀉野食/野飲落佢地件校服到
16.唔小心倒瀉野食/野飲「入」佢地個書包到
17.係鋪頭入面唔小心放左啲未比錢既野入佢個袋到
18.係佢行過個陣唔小心倒屋企啲屎水/污糟水落樓
19.唔小心打開佢書包拉鍊 等佢啲野跌哂出黎
20.唔小心放啲蛇蟲鼠蟻入佢書包
ETC.

大家見到有人咁做既話記得要保護佢地 就算啲警察兒女幾忙幾趕時間都要捉住佢地幫佢地報警 唔好比佢地走
你地知香港警察做野幾有效率架啦 一個人日日等幾次39分鐘都唔係好嘥時間姐
如果佢地自己打電話想搵人幫手 千祈唔好唔小心轟佢部手機落地然後踢到母雷公咁遠

唔撚該 推比所有人睇 政府已經開始推緊緊急法 只係佢無出聲咋
推出所有人睇 比家人 比外國人睇!
Although there is no public announcement, the HK government seems to be pushing out the invocation of emergency regulations ordinance under the table, which basically allow the government to do whatever the fuck they want to the HK people!!


1.深水埗區,黃大仙同沙田入夜已經戒嚴
Sham Shui Po, Wong Tai Sin district is almost at a martial law status at night.

2.狗已經有任何既權利去做任何違反基本法及法治既事
Dirty cops seems to be authorised to act unlawfully

2.1:無理搜身 甚至係巴士/狗鐵公共交通工具搜乘客既身
Stop and search anyone without a valid reason, even the passengers in buses, railway and other public transportation

2.2:30人圍毆一個市民,對女性市民性侵
Commuting police brutality, ex: beating up a citizen with 30 armed cops; sexual harassment towards female citizens.

2.3:以甲由形容市民,不論男女老幼:小至12歲
Calling protesters and even regular citizens cockroaches, from young to old, 12 at the youngest

2.4:無搜索令情況下進行私人地方
Entering private property without a warrant

3.開始限制走資
Starting to regulate and limit capital outflow for political reason

4.開始想封互聯網
Considering to block and control the internet(like how China banned google/fb/ etc...)

5.限制香港市民離境(陳浩天)
Arresting HK citizens who are related to the pro-democracy activity when they try to leave the country for obvious political reason

6.限制自由市場貨品進出
Restricting import and export of free market goods


Ps: 未fc巴打+英文不好試翻, 有問題請提出

1. Curfew has already been imposed by the HKPF in Sham Shui Po district and Wong Tai Sin district.
2. Regulations that clearly violates the basic law have already been made.
2.1 Unreasonable stop and search, HKPF has been performing stop and search even on public transport systems such as MTR and Buses.
2.2 30 police officers jumping on a single citizen, sexually harassing female arrestees.
2.3 Blatantly using hate speech in official documents and public operations. Defining protestors and any opposing citizens as cockroaches, hence justifying their excessive use of force.
2.4 Entering private property without a warrant nor proper reasoning.
3. Proposing to limit internet use by implementing firewalls.
4. Restricting citizens’ departure. (First case: Mr. Chan Ho Tin)
5. Interfering with Hong Kong’s import and export goods.

Despite Carrie Lam not having officially announced the invocation of Emergency Regulations Ordinance, the HK government seems to be effectively implementing it behind the scenes anyways. This gives the government and Police Force unchecked and limitless power to crackdown any dissent in HK!

1. Curfew has essentially been imposed by the HKPF in Sham Shui Po district and Wong Tai Sin district.
2. Actions that clearly violates the Basic Law have escaped all forms of legal repercussions.
2.1 Unreasonable stop and search: HKPF has been performing arbitrary stops and searches with no evidence of crime, sometimes even on public transport systems such as MTR and buses.
2.2 Abuse of force: 30 police officers jumped and beat up a single citizen.
2.3 Sexually harassment against female arrestees.
2.4. Hate speech: Blatant use of hate speech in official documents and public operations. Dehumanising protestors and citizens with opposing political views as cockroaches. This is often used as a tactic to justify excessive force and abuse of power by authoritarian regimes.
2.5. Trespassing: Forcefully entering private property without a warrant nor proper reasoning.
3. A proposal to limit internet access and restrict free flow of information by implementing a city-wide firewall has been put on the table.
4. Restricting citizens’ departure. (First case: Mr. Chan Ho Tin)
5. Interfering with Hong Kong’s import and export goods.

多多指教!
